name: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, turmeric, red chili powder, and garam masala for 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry chicken pieces until cooked through. Set aside.
Heat butter and oil in a pan. Sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes until aromatic.
Add tomato puree and cook on medium heat for 10 minutes until oil separates.
Add cumin, coriander, garam masala, and kashmiri red chili powder. Mix well.
Add heavy cream and mix. Simmer on low heat for 5 minutes.
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy.
Crush kasuri methi between your palms and add to the curry.
Simmer for 10 more minutes. Serve hot with naan or rice.
tips: Use kashmiri red chili for vibrant color without excessive heat.
Let the chicken marinate overnight for deeper flavor.
Adding a pinch of sugar balances the tanginess of tomatoes.

substitutions.heavy cream: coconut cream or cashew paste for dairy-free version
substitutions.chicken breast: paneer or tofu for vegetarian version

name: Dal Tadka
instructions: Wash and soak yellow lentils (toor dal) for 30 minutes.
Pressure cook dal with turmeric, salt, and water for 3-4 whistles.
Heat ghee in a tadka pan. Add cumin and mustard seeds until they splutter.
Add chopped onions and fry until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until mushy.
Add red chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala.
Pour this tadka over the cooked dal and mix well.
Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve hot with rice or roti.
tips: A final tempering of dried red chilies in ghee adds smoky flavor.
For restaurant-style dal, add a small piece of charcoal for smoky aroma.
Adding lemon juice at the end brightens the flavors.

substitutions.yellow lentils: red lentils or split moong dal

name: Vegetable Biryani
instructions: Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Parboil with whole spices and salt until 70% cooked.
Fry thinly sliced onions in oil until deep golden brown (birista). Drain and set aside.
In the same oil, add bay leaves, cloves, cardamom, and cinnamon.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté. Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add mixed vegetables, biryani masala, yogurt, and salt. Cook for 10 minutes.
Layer the partially cooked rice over the vegetable mixture.
Add fried onions, mint leaves, and saffron milk on top.
Seal the pot with a tight lid or dough. Cook on dum (low heat) for 20 minutes.
Let it rest for 10 minutes before opening.
Gently mix and serve with raita and salad.
tips: Soaking saffron in warm milk gives better color and aroma.
Dum cooking (sealed low-heat cooking) is the key to authentic biryani.
Use long-grain aged basmati rice for best results.

name: Masoor Dal Soup
instructions: Rinse red lentils thoroughly.
Heat oil and sauté diced onion and garlic until soft.
Add chopped tomato, cumin, turmeric, and red chili powder.
Add lentils and 3 cups water. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until lentils are completely soft.
Blend half the soup for a creamy texture (optional).
Season with salt and lemon juice.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with bread.
tips: Red lentils cook much faster than other varieties.
A squeeze of lemon at the end is essential for brightness.
Add spinach in the last 5 minutes for extra nutrition.

substitutions.red lentils: split moong dal or masoor dal

name: Paneer Tikka
instructions: Cut paneer, bell peppers, onion, and tomato into chunks.
Mix yogurt with ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, red chili powder, turmeric, cumin, salt, and lemon juice.
Marinate paneer and vegetables in the yogurt mixture for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated paneer and vegetables onto skewers.
Grill in oven at 220°C for 15 minutes, turning once halfway.
Alternatively, cook in a grill pan with light oil.
Sprinkle crushed kasuri methi and squeeze lemon juice on top.
Serve with mint chutney and sliced onion rings.
tips: Longer marination (2-3 hours) yields more flavorful tikka.
Charring slightly gives authentic tandoor flavor.
Adding besan (gram flour) to marinade helps it stick better.

name: Aloo Paratha
instructions: Knead whole wheat flour with salt, oil, and water to form a soft dough. Rest for 20 minutes.
Boil, peel, and mash potatoes completely without lumps.
Mix mashed potato with finely chopped onion, green chili, coriander, cumin, ajwain, red chili powder, amchur, and salt.
Divide dough into balls. Flatten each into a small disc.
Place a portion of potato filling in the center.
Seal the edges and gently roll into a flat round paratha.
Cook on a hot tawa with ghee/oil until golden brown spots appear on both sides.
Serve hot with butter, yogurt, or pickle.
tips: Make sure the filling has no excess moisture to prevent paratha from breaking.
Seal the filling completely before rolling to avoid spilling.
Cook on medium-high heat for crispy parathas.

substitutions.whole wheat flour: all-purpose flour or gluten-free flour blend

name: Chana Masala
instructions: Soak dried chickpeas overnight and pressure cook until tender (or use canned).
Heat oil and add cumin seeds until they splutter.
Add finely chopped onions and fry until dark golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Cook 2 minutes.
Add pureed tomatoes and cook until oil separates.
Add all dry spices: coriander, cumin, turmeric, chana masala, red chili powder.
Add cooked chickpeas and mix well with the masala.
Add 1 cup water and simmer for 15 minutes until gravy thickens.
Add amchur powder, adjust salt and garnish with coriander.
Serve with bhatura, puri, or rice.
tips: Pressing some chickpeas while cooking thickens the gravy naturally.
Adding tea bag while cooking chickpeas gives authentic dark color.
Amchur (dry mango powder) is key for the tangy flavor.

substitutions.chana masala powder: mix of coriander, cumin, amchur, and garam masala

name: Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Use day-old cold rice for best results (freshly cooked rice is too moist).
Beat eggs with a pinch of salt.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat. Scramble eggs and set aside.
In the same wok, add more oil. Fry minced garlic and ginger.
Add diced carrots and peas. Stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add cold rice and toss continuously on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce and sesame oil. Mix thoroughly.
Add scrambled eggs back and fold in.
Garnish with spring onions. Serve immediately.
tips: High heat (wok hei) is crucial for restaurant-style fried rice.
Day-old refrigerated rice separates better and doesn't clump.
Add a splash of rice wine vinegar for extra flavor.

substitutions.soy sauce: tamari for gluten-free, or coconut aminos

name: Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, then immediately transfer to ice water to retain green color.
Blend blanched spinach to a smooth puree.
Cut paneer into cubes and lightly fry until golden. Set aside.
Heat ghee in a pan. Add cumin seeds until they crackle.
Sauté finely chopped onions until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add all spice powders and mix well.
Pour in the spinach puree and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes, cream, and kasuri methi. Simmer 5 more minutes and serve.
tips: Blanching and immediately cooling spinach preserves bright green color.
Do not overcook after adding spinach puree — it loses color.
A touch of cream at the end makes the gravy silky smooth.

substitutions.paneer: tofu or chickpeas for vegan version

name: Masala Chai
instructions: Crush ginger, cardamom pods, cloves, and cinnamon lightly.
Boil water with crushed spices for 2 minutes.
Add tea leaves and boil for 1 minute.
Add milk and sugar.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Strain through a fine sieve into cups.
Serve immediately while hot.
tips: Simmering gently (not boiling hard) after adding milk prevents scalding.
Adjust spice quantities to personal preference.
Strong Assam tea works best for masala chai.

substitutions.milk: oat milk or almond milk for dairy-free

name: Rajma (Red Kidney Bean Curry)
instructions: Soak kidney beans overnight. Pressure cook until soft.
Heat oil in a deep pan. Add cumin seeds and bay leaf.
Fry finely chopped onions until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add pureed tomatoes. Cook until oil separates.
Add all dry spices and mix well.
Add cooked kidney beans with their cooking liquid.
Simmer on medium heat for 20 minutes until gravy thickens.
Adjust salt. Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve with steamed rice (Rajma Chawal).
tips: Never add salt while pressure cooking — it makes beans tough.
Mashing a few beans while cooking creates a thicker, creamier curry.
Rajma tastes better the next day as flavors deepen.

substitutions.garam masala: mix of cumin, coriander, and allspice

name: Banana Pancakes
instructions: Mash ripe bananas thoroughly in a bowl.
Add eggs and whisk together.
Mix in flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.
Gradually add milk to get pancake batter consistency.
Add vanilla extract and mix.
Heat a non-stick pan with butter on medium heat.
Pour a ladle of batter. Cook until bubbles form on surface (2-3 min).
Flip and cook other side until golden.
Serve with maple syrup, honey, or fresh fruit.
tips: Riper bananas make sweeter, more flavorful pancakes.
Don't overmix the batter — lumps are okay.
Let batter rest 5 minutes before cooking for fluffier pancakes.

substitutions.all-purpose flour: oat flour or almond flour for gluten-free
substitutions.eggs: flax egg (1 tbsp flaxseed + 3 tbsp water) for vegan
